IsA: It is a watercolour by British artist and poet Dante Gabriel Rossetti.
HasA: It has left- and right-hand panels showing the lovers together.
It has a central panel showing Dante and the Roman poet Virgil.
It has inscriptions taken from Canto V.
It has relevant quotations in the original Italian around the edge of the composition.
It has a left-hand panel showing the adulterous kiss that condemns the lovers.
It has a central panel depicting two of Rossetti's literary heroes crowned with laurel.
It has a final panel showing the lovers being blown about violently with the wind.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by an archaic, medievalising style.
It is characterized by simple drawing.
It is characterized by generally muted colours.
It is characterized by Francesca's long golden hair looking forward to more sensuous creatures of Rossetti's later works.
UsedFor: It is used for depicting the adulterous love between Paolo Malatesta and Francesca da Rimini.
LocatedIn: It is located in Tate Britain.
OriginatedIn: It originated in Canto V of Dante's Inferno.
CreatedBy: It was created by British artist and poet Dante Gabriel Rossetti.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the left-hand panel showing the adulterous kiss.
It consists of the central panel depicting Virgil and Dante.
It consists of the final panel showing the lovers being blown about by the wind.
PartOf: It is part of the collection at Tate Britain.
KnownFor: It is known for being inspired by Canto V of Dante's Inferno.
It is known for depicting the adulterous love between Paolo Malatesta and Francesca da Rimini.
It is known for its triptych format.
It is known for its archaic, medievalising style.
It is known for the inscriptions and quotations from Dante's Inferno.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Dante Gabriel Rossetti.
It is associated with Dante's Inferno, Canto V.
It is associated with Paolo Malatesta and Francesca da Rimini.
It is associated with the Roman poet Virgil.
It is associated with John Ruskin.
It is associated with the Pre-Raphaelite ideology.
About: It is about the adulterous love between Paolo Malatesta and Francesca da Rimini.
It is about Dante's Inferno, Canto V.
It is about the story of Paolo and Francesca.
It is about the tragic adulterous story of Paolo and Francesca.
It is about the Arthurian knight Sir Lancelot.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Canto V of Dante's Inferno.
It was influenced by Dante Alighieri's Inferno, Canto V.
It was influenced by the story of Paolo and Francesca.
It was influenced by the Arthurian knight Sir Lancelot.
It was influenced by the Pre-Raphaelite ideology.
